checks.coverage_rationale: The portable model and router source bind one terminal owner, bounded children, ambiguity, and trivial skip.

The target-owned topology checker preserves LogicGuard, SourceGuard, and TraceGuard semantic ownership through one ResearchGuard console and rejects retired ids, old module commands, direct imports, bare executable routes, and alternate-success paths.
The native investigation route binds observed sources, semantic support, alternatives, gaps, and ResearchPacket closure.
The native academic route binds bounded evidence children, source-unit provenance, citation fit, and academic final ownership.
The fiction route retains story contribution, continuity, Guard lifecycle, semantic review, real-manuscript identity, and model-prose binding.
The travel route retains source-time, feasibility, fit, reachable fallback, shared reader projection, and actual-guide closure.
The neutral writing contract binds unit contribution, reader state, register, variation, model rows, and exact current artifact bytes without sibling ownership.
The reader boundary is tied to the sanitized brief and actual current artifact, not caller metadata.
The closure source rejects stale, self-reported, process-only, outline-only, and metadata-only substitutes.
The target-owned evaluator accepts the complete obligation set and detects one exact important reader-artifact omission.
The installed projection retains one entrypoint, its portable metadata, current contract authority, and no former runtime surface.

claim_boundary: This manifest binds checks to exact model obligations. Passing checks do not by themselves prove target execution, user-visible quality, full closure, installation, or publication.
